- [x] Verify that the copilot-instructions.md file in the .github directory is created.

- [x] Clarify Project Requirements
  Product direction chosen: a Next.js TypeScript web app prototype for collaborative vibe coding with friend-first usability, GitHub-backed execution, and simple project visibility.

- [x] Scaffold the Project
  The intended Next.js scaffold was prepared for the current directory. Since Node.js and npm are not installed in this environment, the project was created manually with the equivalent base files.

- [x] Customize the Project
  Added a landing page, a collaborative workspace prototype, and product strategy documents aligned to the requested concept.

- [x] Install Required Extensions
  No extensions were specified by the setup information.

- [x] Compile the Project
  All 17 routes compile cleanly via `npm run build`. TypeScript passes, static export generates successfully.

- [x] Create and Run Task
  `npm run build:electron` builds Next.js + packages via electron-builder. `npm run deploy` builds and deploys to install folder.

- [x] Launch the Project
  App runs via `npm run dev:electron` (dev mode) or `debug-start.bat` / `FRESH-START.bat` (production exe).

- [x] Ensure Documentation is Complete
  README.md exists, this file has been updated to reflect current project state, and the HTML comments were removed.

- Keep communication concise and focused.
- Follow development best practices.
